stem: Two lines intersect at point P, forming four angles. One of these angles has measure 110°. A triangle MPN has its vertex at P, and the interior angle of triangle MPN at vertex P is one of the angles formed by the two lines and is supplementary to the 110° angle. If the measure of angle M in triangle MPN is 28°, what is the measure, in degrees, of angle N?
rationale: Two angles forming a straight line are supplementary, so the interior angle of triangle MPN at P measures 180 − 110 = 70°. The three interior angles of a triangle sum to 180°, so ∠N = 180 − 70 − 28 = 82°.
